title: Loading on 5-Meter Granite Walkways: How Active Movers Avoids Obstructions on the New Bole 22 Smart Boulevard
excerpt: 5-meter granite walkways Bole 22 smart boulevard: loading without obstructions, pedestrian priority, dollies and staging tactics Active Movers.
content:
Loading on 5-meter granite walkways: how Active Movers avoids obstructions on the new Bole 22 smart boulevard

New Bole 22 smart boulevard pairs vehicle lanes with five-meter granite walkways—premium urban design that punishes movers who block pedestrian flow or chip stone with reckless dollies. Active Movers treats granite as client asset: runners, edge protection, and zero overnight box piles on civic walkway.

This guide loads safely on Bole 22 five-meter granite walkways without obstructions.

Granite walkway rules

  • No truck tires on walkway—ever.
  • Dolly paths only with masonite or plywood runners.
  • No staged furniture blocking pedestrian width.
  • Chip risk at edges—lift not drag.
  • Security and city enforcement photo fines.

Active Movers obstruction avoidance

Truck stays legal curb lane or compound bay; shuttle hand-carry or cart train; two-person spotter pedestrian side; timed unload bursts not all-day sprawl; complete sweep—no wrap scraps left.

When walkway is only path

Management escort; early morning low pedestrian; barrier cones company branded; apologize route minimal—speed with care not rush damage.

Equipment standards

Rubber wheel dollies; blanket between granite and metal; shoe covers crew; corner guards building lobby continuation same discipline.

Client role

Approve staging sketch; notify building; accept shuttle time honest.

Conclusion

Bole 22 granite walkway loading succeeds with truck off stone, runner-protected dollies, and zero obstruction—smart boulevard moves polish city face not scar it.
author: Active Movers Team
